When we were students, my friends and I rented a cabin ___ the mountains so that we could go hiking. It only cost us £55 for the whole week, not ___ food, of course. One day, my friend Daniel got tired and stopped to rest, saying he’d catch ___ later, but when he still hadn’t returned to the cabin ___ late afternoon, we started getting worried. Luckily, he met some men who lived in the area and they brought him back to the cabin before it got dark.

___ their hair wasn’t actually very long, rock groups such as the Beatles and the Rolling Stones were often criticized as ‘long-haired’ or ‘needing haircuts’ when they first became popular during the early 1960s. At that time men were sometimes considered effeminate if they ___ long hair. The opposite was true for men who grew a beard ___, of course, it was allowed to grow too long. Beards grow faster than hair and need more care. In fact, if the average man never trimmed his beard, it ___ to nearly ten metres in his lifetime. Now, that’s a lot of hair!

___ in most other sports players are usually trying to get the most goals or points ___ win, the opposite is true in golf. In a game of golf, it is the lowest score that wins. Each player must try to get his or her ball in the hole ___ as few shots as possible. Do you sometimes feel anxious or irritable when you’re driving? It may be the smell inside your car ___ is determining how you feel. A recent study of American drivers found that the smell of peppermint or cinnamon improved their performance by reducing anxiety more than 20 per cent. Alertness ___ increased by almost 30 per cent. ___, the smell of cakes or fast food made drivers more irritable and caused them to speed, probably because those smells stimulate hunger ___ make drivers more anxious to get where they’re going sooner.
